name: Nellis AFB
profile: Military-heavy Las Vegas housing snapshot anchored by Nellis AFB, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews with affordability, rent, ownership, and military-fit context.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Cooling but still active; buyers have more leverage than during peak pandemic-era conditions.

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 VA-style purchase assumptions and Las Vegas ownership cost estimates.

notes: Nevada property taxes are generally lower than Texas-style markets, which can help all-in monthly ownership cost.
HOA exposure can vary materially in Las Vegas subdivisions and should be included in true payment comparisons.
Insurance, utilities, summer cooling, and commute patterns should be stress-tested before using purchase price alone as the affordability screen.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: Estimated VA loan all-in payment for Las Vegas area using average home value, 6.2% APR, property tax, insurance, and HOA assumptions.

crime_status.installation: Secure military installation environment with controlled access and major Air Force mission presence.
crime_status.metro_las_vegas: Urban crime patterns vary sharply by neighborhood; buyers should compare North Las Vegas, Summerlin, Henderson, and east-side corridors at the neighborhood level.

school_quality.on_base: Military families typically evaluate nearby Clark County School District options and private/charter alternatives depending on commute and housing location.
school_quality.off_base: School quality varies widely across the Las Vegas metro; Summerlin, Henderson, and selected northwest pockets are often stronger family-screening areas.

rental_vacancy.notes: Las Vegas rental market appears more balanced than peak-pandemic conditions, with rents cooling from prior highs but still elevated versus pre-pandemic levels.

rental_metrics.notes: Based on Las Vegas 2026 rental-market ranges, with apartment averages lower and single-family rentals higher depending on bedroom count and neighborhood.

special_events: Major concerts and conventions on the Las Vegas Strip

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Nellis AFB is a major Air Force hub with advanced combat training, strong military infrastructure, and access to the broader Las Vegas metro housing market.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Las Vegas is more expensive than many traditional military metros, but lower property taxes and cooling home prices can improve the ownership equation for disciplined buyers.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Cooling prices and longer days on market may create negotiation opportunities, especially away from premium west-side and Henderson pockets.

market_bluf.bluf_headline: Cooling Desert Market With Real Leverage
market_bluf.bluf_summary: Nellis families get access to a large, liquid Las Vegas housing market where prices have softened, but all-in ownership still requires careful BAH, commute, HOA, and summer utility stress-testing.

summary_points: Nellis AFB sits inside the Las Vegas metro, a large and liquid housing market with broader options than many military towns.
Home values have cooled year-over-year, which improves buyer leverage compared with peak-market conditions.
Las Vegas remains more expensive than lower-cost military metros, so BAH-to-payment discipline matters.
Lower Nevada property taxes can help offset higher purchase prices, but HOA, insurance, and summer cooling costs still matter.
The best PCS strategy is to compare commute, school quality, HOA exposure, and resale flexibility rather than chasing the lowest list price.
opportunities: Cooling prices and longer days on market can give PCS buyers room to negotiate.
Large metro inventory gives military families more neighborhood choice than isolated base markets.
Lower Nevada property tax rates can support ownership math compared with higher-tax states.
Military-connected demand near Nellis and Creech supports long-term rental relevance.
Single-family rental demand can be durable if the property is located in a practical commute corridor.
risks: Purchase prices remain high enough that some BAH profiles may be better served by renting.
Summer utility costs can materially increase the true monthly housing burden.
HOA dues are common in many Las Vegas subdivisions and should not be ignored.
Neighborhood quality, schools, commute time, and crime exposure vary sharply across the metro.
Premium areas such as Summerlin and Henderson may stretch military affordability faster than North Las Vegas or east/northeast corridors.
buyer_notes: Nellis buyers should evaluate all-in monthly cost, not just the principal-and-interest mortgage estimate.
A lower property tax environment helps, but the Las Vegas purchase price, HOA, insurance, and cooling costs still need to fit inside a safe housing ratio.
North Las Vegas and northeast corridors may improve commute practicality, while Summerlin and Henderson may offer stronger lifestyle appeal at higher prices.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who negotiate, inspect carefully, and avoid emotional overextension.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against the current cooling market rather than old peak-price expectations.
Move-in-ready condition, realistic pricing, and strong presentation matter more when buyers have more inventory to compare.
Homes that serve military commute patterns and family needs can still stand out if positioned correctly.
investor_angles: Nellis and Creech military turnover can support rental demand, but investors should underwrite vacancy and maintenance conservatively.
The Las Vegas market may favor stable cash-flow discipline over aggressive appreciation assumptions.
Single-family homes with practical commute access, moderate HOA exposure, and durable tenant demand are stronger underwriting candidates.
Bedroom-specific rent and purchase bands are more useful than broad metro averages when evaluating military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare HOA, insurance, utilities, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not assume Las Vegas affordability from purchase price alone; summer cooling and HOA can change the answer.
A slightly lower purchase price or lower-HOA property can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near the Nellis and Creech corridors.
Investors should model vacancy, repairs, HOA, insurance, utilities, and turnover conservatively.
The strongest rental candidates are practical homes with good commute access and manageable monthly carrying costs.

market.market_type_summary: Cooling but still active; more buyer leverage than peak market.

base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Nellis AFB is a major combat training and test installation supporting advanced air combat training, Red Flag, and access to the broader Las Vegas housing market.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Nellis AFB is a major Air Force hub with advanced combat training, strong military infrastructure, and access to the broader Las Vegas metro housing market.
base_profile.base_bluf: Nellis families get access to a large, liquid Las Vegas housing market where prices have softened, but all-in ownership still requires careful BAH, commute, HOA, and summer utility stress-testing.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, commute testing, childcare access, and total monthly housing cost before signing near Nellis.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, rental flexibility, and access to daily services around Nellis AFB.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: Lead with BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in payment math for Nellis clients — not generic Las Vegas / North Las Vegas stats alone.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Nellis is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, and practical commute corridors.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Nellis, gate access and traffic timing often matter more than straight-line distance.
primary_commute_factors: Shift schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, and safety screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Traffic and gate choice can still change the real commute.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for families wanting larger homes or specific schools
commute_bands.watchout: Can become painful for early reporting or frequent base errands.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Identify the most realistic primary gate.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window you would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Nellis housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH is a decision filter, not a promise of affordability.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter for Nellis, not a promise of affordability. Push users into PCSUnited financial calculators when the housing gap looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Nellis should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, commute reality, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: School quality varies widely across the Las Vegas metro; Summerlin, Henderson, and selected northwest pockets are often stronger family-screening areas.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.primary_medical_anchor: Nellis Air Force Base medical services
medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: The broader Las Vegas / North Las Vegas metro may offer spouse employment options, but commute, childcare, and credentialing should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Control Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official Nellis Air Force Base gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the same as commute
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access and traffic timing.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Property taxes and insurance shape true payment
pcs_watchouts.message: In this market, taxes (~0.64%) and insurance assumptions must be included in all-in comparisons.

likely_questions: Can I afford to buy near Nellis?

likely_questions: Where do military families usually live?
Which school zones should I research?
buyer_personas.agent_strategy: Compare top Nellis corridors against commute, schools, and all-in monthly cost.
Keep recommendations practical: commute, rent, safety, property condition, and lease flexibility.

common_objections: Affordability gap between BAH and mortgage
recommended_agent_behavior: Always recommend school-zone verification by address.
Use payment math, not just listing price.
Avoid generic Las Vegas / North Las Vegas advice when the user is assigned to Nellis.

source_notes.label: Official Nellis Air Force Base website

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

base.notes: Used for Nellis AFB location, Clark County context, and ZIP 89191.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau QuickFacts / Census Reporter ACS

rent.provider: Zillow Rental Manager / Apartments.com / local Las Vegas rental reports

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: This Nellis AFB file was upgraded to the Lackland-style PCSUnited schema with slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
Las Vegas / North Las Vegas is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context; base-specific market data was retained.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.

market_area.method: Uses surrounding PCS buyer/renter ZIPs, not installation-only ZIP.

housing_market.notes: Sale and rental metrics are aggregated across selected PCS housing-market ZIPs.
